title: Which Operating System is the Best?
body: The OS was the heart, the brain, and the soul of your computer, but I believe this responsibility has now **passed to your browser**. You increasingly own more *gadgets* and access your *email* from many devices, your mobile phone, laptop, work computer, a friend's, or at an *internet* café, so you cannot depend on a single physical device where you have your documents, your *email* client emails, your photos and even your music. I have gotten used to living with multiple Operating Systems, Windows Vista on my Media Center, Ubuntu on this *netbook*, and XP and Mac OS at work, plus the OS your mobile phone uses (Symbian, iPhone, Android ...). In the end what you seek is productivity at work (XP or Mac), entertainment on your Media Center (Vista ??) and usability on your mobile (Android?) Luckily, increasingly all these OS understand each other better and you can connect them between them via ethernet, USB, WiFi, Bluetooth or simply through the internet. All [my email accounts are centralized in Gmail,](http://www.xarop.com/todos-tus-emails-en-gmail/) my contacts on my social networks, my favorite photos on Flickr, my documents on GoogleDocs and on my blogs, my links on gmarks and delicious, my music taste on last.fm, videos on YouTube or Vimeo, etc. so I no longer depend on an operating system or a specific computer, simply on a browser on any device connected to the Internet. Of course, online software is still not very powerful and we don't have good connections everywhere, that's why we still depend on the Operating System to load our professional tools, in my case I would like not to have to depend on Windows or Apple and to be able to work from my browser, there's little left for this to be possible.
